name: Orange Hot and Sweet Mustard
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the dry mustard in a double boiler and do not allow it to come into direct contact with heat.
2. Gradually add the olive oil a little at a time, stirring after each addition to prevent lumps.
3. Stir in the orange zest, cinnamon, and remaining ingredients.
4. Heat covered over simmering water for 15 minutes, scraping the sides occasionally to combine.
5. Allow the mustard mixture to cool completely before transferring it to jars.
6. Refrigerate the mustard. Check consistency after one day; if too thick, thin with a little water as needed.
7. Properly stored, this recipe makes approximately 2 cups and will keep for up to 1 month.
